betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Accelerator Ion Source: Positron Source§f

§aThe Accelerator Ion Source produces particle beams from a fluid input.§f
The §bAccelerator Ion Source§f (`qmd:accelerator_source`, item `qmd:accelerator_source:0`, also called the §bPositron Source§f in this quest) is a single block that consumes a fluid from an adjacent tank and emits a stream of particles. The emitted species, output rate, and required fluid are defined per-source in `lach_01298.qmd.recipes.AcceleratorSourceRecipes.java`.

§6Example — Boron-11 Ion Source§f:
Per `AcceleratorSourceRecipes.java:31`:
- Fluid Input: §bDiborane§f (`liquid:diborane`)
- Particle Output: §bboron_ion§f (Boron-11: 5 protons + 6 neutrons + 4 electrons), at rate `QMDConstants.ionSourceOutput`
- No item input required

§6Crafting Recipe§f:
Per the QMD crafting recipe handler, Accelerator Ion Sources are crafted in a vanilla §bminecraft.crafting§f grid from:
- §bAdvanced Plating§f (`nuclearcraft:part:1`)
- §bAccelerator Casing§f (`qmd:accelerator_casing`)

§6Direct Upgrades§f:
- The §bAccelerator Laser Ion Source§f (`qmd:accelerator_laser_ion_source`) is an upgrade tier that produces a different particle species at higher efficiency. It uses the same kind of source recipe but is unlocked by a downstream quest.

§6Placement in Multiblocks§f:
Ion Sources are placed as part of the §bLinear Accelerator (LINAC)§f multiblock. Each source occupies a specific port in the accelerator structure; the emitted particles are then accelerated by §bRF Cavities§f and focused by §bQuadrupole Electromagnets§f.

§eTip§f: Always read the JEI category for the specific source you build — the source determines the particle species the LINAC will accelerate, which in turn determines what downstream recipes will work in your §bTarget Chamber§f.
